response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial and distributed-systems experience through GISCollective and high-throughput microservices work at Sociomantic, but the core technical requirement — modern C++ or Rust proficiency in a commercial or production context — is critically unmet. C++ appears only in a 2014-2015 role at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project, both over a decade ago. The primary professional stack across the last five years is TypeScript and Node.js. The geospatial work, while real, is oriented toward data collection and mapping for NGOs rather than routing-tile generation, traffic integration, or navigation pipeline optimization. The seniority gap between what the role demands and what the candidate demonstrates in the required technologies is too wide for a mid-senior individual contributor position.
strengths: Genuine geospatial domain exposure through GISCollective and Green Map GIS projects, including a FOSS4G conference talk
Demonstrated high-throughput, low-latency systems experience at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second with async I/O and caching
Strong engineering discipline: TDD advocacy, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and open-source tooling contributions
concerns: C++ experience is limited to a single role ending in 2015 and a thesis project — no modern, production-grade C++ or Rust anywhere in the resume
Geospatial work is focused on data collection and visualization for NGOs, not routing pipelines, tile optimization, or navigation accuracy
Current and recent roles are entirely TypeScript/Node.js full-stack, with no evident path toward the systems-level stack this role requires
